items.prompt: identifies an angle as the amount of turn between two rays or directions
items.expected_evidence: show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner completes the task accurately and justifies the choice.

items.prompt: compares angles as larger, smaller, or equal based on turn size rather than side length
items.expected_evidence: compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the reasoning using correct geometric language or supported demonstration.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to benchmark_turn_instability.
items.expected_evidence: use spatial language to describe clockwise and counterclockwise turning situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the mistaken rule and states the correct reasoning.

items.prompt: succeeds across body movement, clock-like turns, and drawn angle comparisons
items.expected_evidence: connect angle size to changes in direction in familiar contexts
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner succeeds in a less-familiar context without loss of concept integrity.
